prompts.seed:
Classify the given text into one of these categories: alarm, apr, balance, book_flight, book_hotel, calculator, calories, car_rental, cook_time, credit_limit, credit_score, definition, direct_deposit, freeze_account, lost_luggage, meeting_schedule, pay_bill, payday, pto_request, recipe, report_fraud, report_lost_card, restaurant_reservation, restaurant_reviews, rewards_balance, taxes, timer, transfer, travel_suggestion, weather.

Respond with exactly one category name from the list above and nothing else.
prompts.x1:
Classify the given text into one of these categories: alarm, apr, balance, book_flight, book_hotel, calculator, calories, car_rental, cook_time, credit_limit, credit_score, definition, direct_deposit, freeze_account, lost_luggage, meeting_schedule, pay_bill, payday, pto_request, recipe, report_fraud, report_lost_card, restaurant_reservation, restaurant_reviews, rewards_balance, taxes, timer, transfer, travel_suggestion, weather.

Key distinctions:
- pto_request: requesting time off from work (vacation requests, leave requests)
- travel_suggestion: asking for travel destination ideas or recommendations (NOT requesting time off)
- payday: asking when the user gets paid or about their pay schedule
- direct_deposit: setting up or managing automatic salary deposits (employer paying employees)
- book_hotel: searching for or booking a hotel room (focus on accommodation booking)
- restaurant_reviews: reading reviews about restaurants (NOT hotels)
- pay_bill: paying bills or debts owed by the user
- direct_deposit: receiving salary payments from an employer

When text mentions "vacation" in the context of requesting time off work, classify as pto_request.
When text mentions salary deposits or when paychecks arrive, classify as payday (unless it's about employer systems paying employees, which is direct_deposit).
When text is about finding hotels with reviews, focus on the primary intent (booking) and classify as book_hotel.
When text is about employer payment systems or paying employees, classify as direct_deposit.

Respond with exactly one category name from the list above and nothing else.
